q1.content: To start, I would like to begin with a big question: tell me the story of your life. Start from the beginning -- from your childhood, to education, to family and relationships, and to any major life events you may have had.
q1.requirement: Learn as much as you can about the interviewee's life experience, and their personal background. Be respectful but curious as you hear about their story.

q2.content: What hobbies or interests did you have growing up, and what do you enjoy doing now?
q2.requirement: Learn in detail what hobbies and interests the person has. Be respectful but curious as you hear about their story.

q3.content: What brought you to MIT and what do you hope to get out of your time here?
q3.requirement: Learn why the interviewee chose MIT, and what they hope to get out of their time here. Be respectful but curious as you hear about their story.
